message:
fix(circuit_breaker): clamp half_open_max_probes to minimum of 1

Add MIN_HALF_OPEN_PROBES constant and normalize_half_open_max_probes()
helper to ensure that setting half_open_max_probes to 0 does not silently
create a circuit breaker that can never leave the half-open state (zero
probes means no trial requests, so recovery is permanently blocked).

The new clamping range is [1, 16_777_215] (MIN..=MAX). Both the builder
and the direct constructor now funnel through the same normalization
function, eliminating a code duplication between CircuitBreaker::new()
and CircuitBreakerPolicyBuilder::half_open_max_probes().

Also includes rustfmt 2024 import reordering (Arc before atomic).

Tests added:
- builder_clamps_zero_probes_to_minimum: verifies builder normalization
- constructor_clamps_zero_probes_to_minimum_semantics: verifies the
  circuit breaker transitions correctly through Open→HalfOpen→HalfOpenFull
  with the minimum probe count of 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
